full_name: Bahrain Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) — Law No. 30 of 2018

controls.description: Organizations processing personal data must register as a data controller with the Bahrain PDPA.
controls.logic: FAIL if no pdpa_registration_number exists in the compliance register, or if registration_status = 'EXPIRED' or 'PENDING'.

controls.requirement: Explicit Consent Before Processing Sensitive Data
controls.description: Explicit, written consent must be obtained from data subjects before processing sensitive personal data (health, religion, biometrics).
controls.logic: FAIL if any sensitive_personal_data record has consent_type != 'EXPLICIT' or consent_obtained = FALSE.

controls.description: Data subjects have the right to access their personal data and must receive a response within 30 days.
controls.logic: FAIL if any dsar_record has response_date - request_date > 30, or if response_date = NULL for requests older than 30 days.

controls.description: Data controllers must implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures to protect personal data.
controls.logic: FAIL if any personal_data table has encryption_enabled = FALSE, or if access_control_policy_documented = FALSE.

controls.requirement: Cross-Border Transfers Only to Adequate Countries or with Safeguards
controls.description: Personal data may only be transferred outside Bahrain to countries with adequate protection or with appropriate safeguards.
controls.logic: FAIL if any data_transfer has destination_country NOT IN bahrain_adequate_countries AND transfer_mechanism = NULL.

controls.description: The PDPA must be notified of any personal data breach that may harm data subjects.
controls.logic: FAIL if any data_breach with harm_risk = TRUE has pdpa_notification_sent = FALSE, or if notification_date is more than 72 hours after detection.
